analysis: Orthogonal Docking Validation (AutoDock Vina vs DiffDock)

methods.diffdock: DiffDock v1/v2.2 (ML-based, geometric diffusion on protein surface)

CORO1C.note: Best available experimental structure for coronin family

SMN2.note: WARNING: Multi-chain asymmetric unit created artifact scores. Needs single-chain re-docking.

UBA1.note: Large multi-domain structure, center computed from all CA atoms

docking_results.note: 4-AP is a very small molecule (MW 94). Weak Vina score expected for such small ligands. Vina systematically underestimates small-molecule binding.
ARTIFACT: Score < -20 kcal/mol is physically impossible. Caused by multi-chain PDB (4A4G has 12+ chains). Must re-dock with single chain A only.
Moderate binding affinity for riluzole against UBA1. Riluzole (MW 234) shows consistent binding across poses.
ARTIFACT: Same multi-chain issue as riluzole-SMN2. Must re-dock with chain A only.

4AP_CORO1C.note: CORO1C identified via network analysis (+0.251 score), not previously docked with DiffDock

4AP_SMN2.note: Top binder in v1 (confidence > 0 is excellent), strong in v2.2

riluzole_UBA1.note: Not previously docked against UBA1 with DiffDock

consensus.consensus_note: NEW PAIR. Vina score of -3.98 is at the weak/moderate boundary. For a very small molecule like 4-AP (MW 94, only 7 heavy atoms), Vina systematically gives lower scores due to fewer van der Waals contacts. This does NOT mean no binding -- Vina has known limitations with fragment-sized molecules. Network analysis score of +0.251 remains the stronger evidence. Recommend experimental validation (SPR/ITC).
consensus.action: Experimental validation needed. The network analysis evidence (+0.251) is more informative than Vina for this interaction.

consensus.consensus_note: ARTIFACT: Vina score invalid due to multi-chain PDB. DiffDock confidence of -0.3 indicates moderate binding. Needs re-docking with single-chain SMN Tudor domain.
consensus.action: Re-dock with chain A of 4A4G only, or use AlphaFold structure when available.

consensus.consensus_note: NEW PAIR. Riluzole shows moderate Vina binding to UBA1 (-4.6 kcal/mol). Riluzole is an approved ALS drug (MW 234) with known neuroprotective properties. Moderate binding suggests potential off-target interaction worth investigating.
consensus.action: Compare with UBA1 inhibitors. Run DiffDock for UBA1-riluzole to get ML-based consensus.

consensus.consensus_note: ARTIFACT: Vina score invalid. DiffDock v1 confidence of +0.1 (excellent) and v2.2 confidence of -0.28 (strong) both support 4-AP binding to SMN2. This is our strongest hit by DiffDock.
consensus.action: DiffDock evidence is strong. Re-dock with Vina using single-chain for full orthogonal validation.

key_findings: 4-AP vs CORO1C: First physics-based docking. Vina -3.98 kcal/mol (weak-to-moderate, but expected for MW=94 fragment). Network analysis remains primary evidence.
Riluzole vs UBA1: NEW moderate binding (Vina -4.6 kcal/mol). First orthogonal evidence for this pair.
SMN2 dockings: ARTIFACT from multi-chain PDB. Must re-run with single chain.
limitations: SMN2 (PDB 4A4G) has 12+ chains in the asymmetric unit, causing unrealistic scores (-93 kcal/mol). Solution: extract chain A only.
AlphaFold DB was unavailable during this run (HTTP 404/500). Would provide better single-chain structures.
Vina underestimates binding for very small molecules (< 150 Da) like 4-AP due to fewer hydrophobic contacts.
Blind docking (whole-protein box) is less accurate than site-directed docking with known active site.
next_steps: Re-dock SMN2 pairs with chain A only from 4A4G (or AlphaFold when available)
Run DiffDock for riluzole vs UBA1 and 4-AP vs CORO1C for full cross-validation
Use focused docking box on known binding sites (Tudor domain aromatic cage for SMN2)
Consider MM-GBSA rescoring for more accurate free energy estimates

vina_score_guide.note: Scores are size-dependent. Small fragments (MW < 150) rarely exceed -5.0 even with real binding.
